articles.headline: India Surpasses Initial Renewable Energy Targets, Eyes Ambitious New Goals for 2030
articles.explainer:
• India has significantly exceeded its initial renewable energy capacity targets, primarily driven by solar and wind power expansion.
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced new, more ambitious targets for 2030, aligning with India's commitment to climate action.
• This achievement is attributed to robust policy support, financial incentives, and technological advancements.
• The accelerated transition strengthens India's energy security and reduces reliance on fossil fuel imports.
• It positions India as a global leader in renewable energy deployment, enhancing its climate diplomacy efforts.
gsSubTopics: Energy
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy
articles.source: Ministry of New & Renewable Energy / PTI
prelimsPoints: India's current installed renewable energy capacity (approximate for 2025: e.g., 200 GW+).
New ambitious target for 2030 (e.g., 500 GW non-fossil fuel capacity).
Key schemes: PLI for Solar PV modules, PM-KUSUM, National Green Hydrogen Mission.
International Solar Alliance (ISA) headquarters is in Gurugram, India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted benefits of India's accelerated renewable energy transition (economic, environmental, geopolitical).
Analyze the policy framework and incentives driving this growth, including financial mechanisms and technological push.
Examine challenges and opportunities in achieving the new ambitious targets (grid integration, energy storage solutions, land acquisition, financing models).
Role of renewable energy in India's climate diplomacy, global leadership, and achieving Net-Zero targets.
articles.imageDescription: A vast solar power plant array under a clear sky, with transmission lines in the background, symbolizing India's renewable energy push.
articles.trivia: India's Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan is one of the largest solar parks in the world, contributing significantly to the nation's solar capacity.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Petitions Challenging Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court is set to hear a batch of petitions challenging various provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023.
• Petitioners argue that certain clauses infringe upon fundamental rights, particularly the right to privacy and freedom of speech.
• Key concerns include the broad powers granted to the government for exemptions and the grievance redressal mechanism.
• The government maintains that the Act balances individual rights with legitimate state interests and promotes a safe digital ecosystem.
• This hearing is crucial for clarifying the legal framework for data governance in India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
Right to Privacy
articles.source: The Hindu / Live Law
prelimsPoints: Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023: key features, purpose.
Data Protection Board of India: its role and powers.
Fundamental Rights involved: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty), Article 19 (Freedom of Speech and Expression).
Puttaswamy Judgement (2017) on Right to Privacy.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the balance struck by the DPDP Act between individual privacy rights and state/commercial interests.
Discuss the potential implications of the Supreme Court's decision on India's digital economy and governance.
Examine the challenges in implementing a comprehensive data protection framework in a diverse digital landscape like India's.
Compare India's DPDP Act with global data protection regulations like GDPR.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on law books in a courtroom setting, symbolizing the Supreme Court's role in legal interpretations.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'Right to be forgotten' is a key provision in data protection laws globally, allowing individuals to request deletion of their personal data under certain conditions.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns, Focus on Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged in its latest review.
• This decision comes as retail inflation shows signs of moderation, reducing immediate pressure for rate hikes.
• The MPC's stance reflects a continued focus on supporting economic growth while ensuring inflation remains within the target band.
• Analysts suggest that global economic uncertainties and domestic demand conditions influenced the cautious approach.
• Future policy actions will depend on evolving inflation trajectories, global commodity prices, and domestic growth impulses.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
articles.source: RBI / Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Repo Rate: Definition and its role in monetary policy.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC): Composition, objectives (inflation target: 4% +/- 2%).
Types of inflation: Headline vs. Core inflation.
Quantitative vs. Qualitative tools of monetary policy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate in the current economic scenario.
Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Examine the interplay between monetary policy and fiscal policy in steering India's economy.
Evaluate the impact of global economic factors (e.g., crude oil prices, global interest rates) on RBI's monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: A graph showing a steady or declining inflation curve with a rising GDP curve, symbolizing economic stability and growth.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in India in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in fixing the monetary policy.

articles.headline: India Hosts 5th IORA Summit, Emphasizes Maritime Security and Blue Economy
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the 5th Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA) Summit, bringing together member states for high-level discussions.
• A key focus was enhancing maritime security, combating piracy, and ensuring freedom of navigation in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• Discussions also centered on promoting the 'Blue Economy' through sustainable exploitation of marine resources and ocean governance.
• India reiterated its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, aligning with its SAGAR (Security And Growth for All in the Region) vision.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration outlining collaborative efforts on disaster management, renewable ocean energy, and cultural exchanges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
articles.source: Ministry of External Affairs / PTI
prelimsPoints: IORA: Establishment, objectives, member countries.
SAGAR Vision: India's policy in the Indian Ocean Region.
Blue Economy: its components and significance.
Geostrategic importance of the Indian Ocean.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing strategic importance in the Indian Ocean Region and its role in IORA.
Discuss the challenges to maritime security in the IOR and how regional cooperation can address them.
Examine the potential of the Blue Economy for sustainable development and economic growth for IORA member states.
Evaluate how India's 'Act East' policy and Indo-Pacific vision align with its engagement in IORA.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized map of the Indian Ocean with member countries of IORA highlighted, showing shipping routes and naval vessels.
articles.trivia: IORA was established in 1997 and currently has 23 member states and 10 dialogue partners, aiming to promote regional cooperation and sustainable development within the Indian Ocean rim.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Landing System for Future Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a critical test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) landing system.
• The test involved a precise autonomous landing demonstration, a crucial step towards developing a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) RLV.
• This technology aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches by enabling repeated use of rocket components.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) program is a key part of India's vision for future space exploration and commercial space services.
• Successful development will enhance India's competitiveness in the global space market and support human spaceflight missions like Gaganyaan.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space
articles.source: ISRO / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: RLV-TD program: Objectives and stages (Hypersonic Flight Experiment (HEX), Landing Experiment (LEX), Return Flight Experiment (REX), Scramjet Propulsion Experiment (SPEX)).
Significance of reusable launch vehicles for space missions.
Gaganyaan mission: India's human spaceflight program.
ISRO's other major upcoming missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic and economic significance of developing Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India.
Analyze how RLV technology can contribute to making space access more affordable and sustainable.
Examine the challenges involved in developing and deploying RLV systems, including material science and autonomous guidance.
Evaluate India's position in the global space race with indigenous technological advancements like RLV and Gaganyaan.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) making a controlled landing on a runway.
articles.trivia: The concept of reusable rockets was pioneered by SpaceX with its Falcon 9, significantly reducing launch costs and increasing launch frequency.

articles.headline: Project Tiger Celebrates 52 Years, New Census Shows Steady Growth in Tiger Population
articles.explainer:
• Project Tiger, India's flagship wildlife conservation program, marked its 52nd anniversary, demonstrating remarkable success.
• The latest tiger census data, released by the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA), indicates a steady increase in the wild tiger population.
• This growth is attributed to stringent anti-poaching measures, habitat restoration, and community involvement in conservation efforts.
• The success of Project Tiger is vital for maintaining ecological balance and preserving biodiversity in India's forests.
• Challenges remain, including habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, and the need for continuous monitoring and protection.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
articles.source: NTCA / Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger: Launched year (1973), objectives.
NTCA: Statutory body, functions.
Current estimated tiger population (approximate plausible value for 2025).
Key Tiger Reserves in India.
Conservation status of Tiger (IUCN Red List).
mainsPoints: Evaluate the strategies and successes of Project Tiger in conserving the tiger population in India.
Discuss the ecological significance of tigers as apex predators and their role in maintaining ecosystem health.
Analyze the persistent challenges in tiger conservation, including habitat loss, poaching, and human-wildlife conflict.
Suggest measures for strengthening tiger conservation efforts and ensuring long-term sustainability.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger walking through dense green forest undergrowth, representing successful conservation efforts.
articles.trivia: India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, making its conservation efforts globally significant.

articles.headline: Centre Proposes Amendments to Forest (Conservation) Act Rules to Streamline Clearances
articles.explainer:
• The Union Environment Ministry has proposed amendments to the rules governing the Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980.
• The proposed changes aim to streamline the process for granting forest clearances for developmental projects, reducing delays.
• Critics express concerns that these amendments might dilute environmental safeguards and accelerate deforestation.
• The government argues that the amendments are necessary to balance conservation with the demands of infrastructure development and economic growth.
• Public consultations are underway, inviting feedback from stakeholders before finalization.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
articles.source: The Indian Express / Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980: purpose and key provisions.
Role of Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Concept of Compensatory Afforestation Fund Management and Planning Authority (CAMPA).
Difference between 'forest land' and 'recorded forest area'.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the potential impacts of the proposed amendments to the Forest (Conservation) Act rules on environmental protection and economic development.
Discuss the challenges in balancing conservation needs with infrastructure and industrial development in India.
Examine the role of public participation and environmental impact assessments in ensuring sustainable development projects.
Suggest measures to strengthen forest governance while facilitating necessary development.
articles.imageDescription: A split image showing a dense forest on one side and an infrastructure project (e.g., highway) on the other, symbolizing the conflict between development and conservation.
articles.trivia: The Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980, was enacted to check deforestation and ensure that forest land is not diverted for non-forest purposes without prior approval from the Central Government.

articles.headline: Gati Shakti Master Plan Accelerates Infrastructure Development, Boosts Logistics Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• The PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan has significantly accelerated the pace of multi-modal infrastructure development across India.
• The integrated platform facilitates coordinated planning and execution of projects, reducing delays and cost overruns.
• This initiative aims to improve logistics efficiency, enhance connectivity, and reduce transportation costs for businesses.
• Key achievements include faster approvals, optimal resource utilization, and identification of critical infrastructure gaps.
• The plan is crucial for boosting India's economic competitiveness and achieving its vision of becoming a $5 trillion economy.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
Growth and development
articles.source: NITI Aayog / Ministry of Commerce & Industry
prelimsPoints: PM Gati Shakti: Launch year, objectives, nodal ministry.

Role of GIS mapping in project planning.
Significance of logistics sector for India's economy.
mainsPoints: Analyze how the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan is transforming India's infrastructure landscape and logistics sector.
Discuss the benefits of integrated planning and execution of infrastructure projects under Gati Shakti.

Evaluate the role of Gati Shakti in achieving India's economic growth targets and enhancing global competitiveness.
articles.imageDescription: A composite image showing various infrastructure elements like roads, railways, ports, and airports converging, symbolizing multi-modal connectivity.
articles.trivia: The Gati Shakti platform integrates 16 ministries, including Railways, Roads, Petroleum & Gas, Power, Telecom, Shipping, and Aviation, for synchronized project planning and execution.

articles.headline: India, GCC Nations Deepen Energy and Trade Ties, FTA Talks Progressing
articles.explainer:
• India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) nations are strengthening their strategic partnership, focusing on energy security and trade.
• High-level delegations met to review progress on a proposed Free Trade Agreement (FTA), with significant advancements reported.
• Discussions also covered diversifying trade beyond traditional oil and gas, including food security, technology, and renewable energy.
• The GCC remains a crucial partner for India's energy needs and a major destination for Indian expatriates and investments.
• Enhanced cooperation aims to create a more resilient and integrated economic corridor between the two regions.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
articles.source: Ministry of External Affairs / Gulf News
prelimsPoints: GCC: Member countries, objectives.
India's energy import basket composition.
Significance of Indian diaspora in GCC countries.
Recent FTAs signed by India (e.g., UAE, Australia).
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted nature of India's relationship with GCC countries, encompassing energy, trade, and diaspora.
Discuss the strategic importance of a robust India-GCC FTA for both regions, particularly in diversifying economies.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in enhancing cooperation, including geopolitical shifts and global energy transition.
Evaluate the role of the Indian diaspora in strengthening bilateral ties and contributing to India's economy through remittances.
articles.imageDescription: A handshake between two figures, one representing India and the other the GCC, with oil rigs and cargo ships in the background, symbolizing trade and energy cooperation.
articles.trivia: The GCC is a political and economic union of Arab states bordering the Persian Gulf, including Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates.

articles.headline: Indian Army Inducts Advanced Indigenous Artillery Systems, Boosts Border Preparedness
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Army has formally inducted several advanced indigenous artillery gun systems, bolstering its operational capabilities.
• These systems, developed under the 'Make in India' initiative, enhance firepower and precision strike capabilities, particularly in mountainous terrain.
• The induction is part of a broader push towards defense indigenization and reducing reliance on foreign arms imports.
• It significantly strengthens India's preparedness along its borders, especially the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• This move underscores India's commitment to self-reliance in defense manufacturing and modernizing its armed forces.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Science and Technology- indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
articles.source: Ministry of Defence / DRDO
prelimsPoints: Major indigenous artillery systems (e.g., ATAGS, Vajra-T).
Key initiatives for defense indigenization (e.g., 'Make in India' in Defense, Negative Import List).
Role of DRDO in defense research and development.
Different types of artillery and their strategic uses.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of defense indigenization for India's national security and geopolitical standing.
Discuss the role of indigenous artillery systems in enhancing the Indian Army's operational capabilities, especially in challenging terrains.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in achieving complete self-reliance in defense manufacturing.
Evaluate the impact of defense modernization on India's border management and deterrence capabilities.
articles.imageDescription: An indigenous artillery gun system being test-fired in a desert or mountainous region, with soldiers observing.
articles.trivia: The Advanced Towed Artillery Gun System (ATAGS) is an indigenous 155mm, 52 calibre artillery gun developed by DRDO, which holds the world record for the longest range in its class.

articles.headline: National Health Mission Launches New Initiative for Non-Communicable Disease Prevention
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Mission (NHM) has rolled out a new nationwide initiative focused on the prevention and control of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs).
• The program targets common NCDs like diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, and cancers through early screening and lifestyle modifications.
• It emphasizes community-level awareness, health promotion, and strengthening primary healthcare infrastructure.
• NCDs pose a significant public health challenge in India, contributing to a large burden of morbidity and mortality.
• The initiative aims to reduce the NCD burden and improve the overall health outcomes of the population.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
articles.source: Ministry of Health & Family Welfare / PIB
keyTerms: Ayushman Bharat Health and Wellness Centres (AB-HWCs)
prelimsPoints: National Health Mission (NHM): objectives, components (NRHM, NUHM).
Common NCDs and their risk factors.
Ayushman Bharat: PM-JAY and Health and Wellness Centres (HWCs).
Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) related to health (SDG 3).
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing burden of Non-Communicable Diseases in India and their socio-economic implications.
Analyze the strategies adopted by the National Health Mission to prevent and control NCDs, focusing on primary healthcare.
Examine the role of lifestyle changes, public awareness, and technological interventions in combating NCDs.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving effective NCD control across diverse populations in India.
articles.imageDescription: A group of diverse people participating in a health awareness campaign, with health workers providing information on NCD prevention.
articles.trivia: NCDs account for over 60% of all deaths in India, with cardiovascular diseases, cancers, chronic respiratory diseases, and diabetes being the leading causes.

articles.headline: Fintech Sector Sees Robust Growth, Regulatory Sandbox Expands Scope
articles.explainer:
• India's Fintech sector continues its robust growth trajectory, driven by digital adoption and innovative solutions.
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has expanded the scope of its regulatory sandbox to foster further innovation in emerging areas.
• This expansion aims to test new products and services in a controlled environment, ensuring consumer protection and financial stability.
• Key growth drivers include digital payments, lending, wealth management, and insurance tech (insurtech).
• India's digital public infrastructure (UPI, Aadhaar) provides a strong foundation for Fintech expansion and financial inclusion.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: RBI / NASSCOM
prelimsPoints: Regulatory Sandbox: concept, objectives, and examples of areas covered.
UPI: features, benefits, volume of transactions.
Key initiatives promoting digital payments in India (e.g., BHIM, RuPay).
Role of NPCI (National Payments Corporation of India).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the rapid growth of the Fintech sector in India and its impact on financial inclusion.
Discuss the role of regulatory sandboxes in fostering innovation while mitigating risks in the financial sector.
Examine the opportunities and challenges for Fintech companies in leveraging India's digital public infrastructure.
Evaluate the potential of Fintech to transform traditional banking and financial services in India.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing interconnected digital payment icons (mobile, card, QR code) around a globe, representing global fintech growth.
articles.trivia: India's UPI (Unified Payments Interface) is one of the world's most successful real-time payment systems, facilitating billions of transactions monthly.

articles.headline: Coastal Erosion Mitigation Efforts Intensify Along Indian Coastline
articles.explainer:
• Government agencies have intensified efforts to combat coastal erosion, which poses a significant threat to India's coastline and coastal communities.
• New projects involving natural solutions like mangrove plantations and artificial reefs are being implemented alongside engineered structures.
• Coastal erosion is exacerbated by rising sea levels, extreme weather events, and unsustainable human activities.
• The initiatives aim to protect vital infrastructure, preserve biodiversity, and safeguard the livelihoods of coastal populations.
• Integrated Coastal Zone Management (ICZM) plans are being updated to address climate change impacts more effectively.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Geography: Salient features of world’s physical geography
articles.source: National Centre for Coastal Research / Ministry of Earth Sciences

Importance of mangrove ecosystems.
Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) Notification: purpose and categories.
Role of National Centre for Coastal Research (NCCR).
mainsPoints: Discuss the major causes and impacts of coastal erosion on India's coastline and its socio-economic implications.
Analyze the effectiveness of various mitigation strategies, including both engineered and nature-based solutions.
Examine the challenges in implementing Integrated Coastal Zone Management (ICZM) plans in a densely populated coastal region.
Suggest policy recommendations for enhancing coastal resilience in the face of climate change and sea-level rise.
articles.imageDescription: A coastline showing a combination of natural barriers like mangroves and engineered structures like seawalls protecting against ocean waves.
articles.trivia: Mangrove forests are highly effective natural barriers against coastal erosion and storm surges, also serving as crucial breeding grounds for marine life.

articles.headline: Discussions on Electoral Reforms Gain Momentum Ahead of Key State Elections
articles.explainer:
• Debates on electoral reforms have intensified in India, with political parties and civil society organizations advocating for changes.
• Key areas of discussion include campaign finance transparency, voter registration processes, and the use of electronic voting machines (EVMs).
• The timing is crucial as several key state elections are slated for the upcoming months, bringing electoral integrity into focus.
• Proposals range from state funding of elections to stricter regulations on hate speech during campaigns.
• The Election Commission of India continues to push for measures to ensure free, fair, and transparent elections.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Electoral Reforms

mainsPoints: Discuss the need for comprehensive electoral reforms in India to strengthen democratic processes and ensure free and fair elections.

Evaluate the impact of money power, criminalization of politics, and hate speech on India's electoral democracy.
articles.imageDescription: A ballot box with a hand inserting a vote, surrounded by symbols of different political parties, representing the democratic electoral process.
articles.trivia: India's Election Commission is one of the world's largest election management bodies, responsible for conducting elections for Parliament, State Legislatures, and the offices of President and Vice-President.

articles.headline: India's AI Mission Focuses on Ethical AI Development and Skilling Initiatives
articles.explainer:
• The India AI Mission is increasingly emphasizing ethical considerations in the development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence.
• Policy discussions are centered on creating a framework for responsible AI, addressing concerns like bias, transparency, and accountability.
• Simultaneously, the mission is boosting skilling initiatives to equip the workforce with AI-related competencies.
• The goal is to leverage AI for economic growth and social good while mitigating potential risks and ensuring inclusivity.
• India aims to become a global hub for ethical and responsible AI innovation, aligning with its digital leadership ambitions.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.

articles.trivia: India is one of the few countries to have a dedicated national strategy for Artificial Intelligence, aiming for 'AI for All'.

articles.headline: India Pledges Strong Support for UN Peacekeeping Operations, Calls for Reforms
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its strong commitment to United Nations Peacekeeping Operations (UNPKOs), emphasizing its role as a leading troop-contributing country.
• At a recent UN forum, India called for comprehensive reforms within UNPKOs, including enhanced safety for peacekeepers and clear mandates.
• Concerns were raised regarding the underrepresentation of troop-contributing countries in decision-making processes.
• India highlighted its long-standing tradition of contributing to global peace and security through UN missions.
• The call for reforms aims to make UNPKOs more effective, responsive, and accountable in addressing complex global conflicts.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
India and its neighborhood- relations
articles.source: Permanent Mission of India to UN / PTI

Evaluate how India's engagement in UNPKOs aligns with its broader foreign policy objectives and its aspiration for a permanent seat in the UNSC.

articles.trivia: India has contributed over 260,000 troops to 49 UN Peacekeeping Missions since 1948, with a significant number of Indian peacekeepers having made the supreme sacrifice in the line of duty.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing Attracts Major Investment
articles.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage has successfully attracted significant domestic and international investments.
• This scheme aims to establish giga-factories for battery manufacturing in India, reducing import dependence for critical components.
• It is crucial for India's electric vehicle (EV) transition, renewable energy storage, and achieving energy security goals.
• The Ministry of Heavy Industries reported substantial progress in project implementation and capacity building.
• The PLI scheme is a cornerstone of India's 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives in strategic sectors.

Science and Technology- indigenization of technology and developing new technology

articles.trivia: Advanced Chemistry Cells (ACCs) are the new generation of advanced storage technologies that can store electric energy as electrochemical energy and convert it back to electric energy as and when required.

articles.headline: Government Launches National Digital Library 2.0 to Enhance Access to Educational Resources
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education launched the National Digital Library of India (NDLI) 2.0, an upgraded version of the existing platform.
• The enhanced platform offers a vast repository of educational resources, including textbooks, research papers, and audio-visual content.
• It aims to provide equitable access to quality education for students, researchers, and lifelong learners across the country.
• NDLI 2.0 incorporates advanced AI-driven search capabilities and personalized learning pathways.
• This initiative is a crucial step towards realizing the goals of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, focusing on digital education.

Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation

mainsPoints: Discuss how digital libraries like NDLI 2.0 can bridge the educational divide and promote equitable access to knowledge in India.

articles.headline: Ganga Rejuvenation Efforts Enter Phase III, Focus on Tributaries and Sustainable Management
articles.explainer:
• The Namami Gange Programme has entered its third phase, expanding its focus beyond the main stem of the Ganga to its tributaries.
• The new phase emphasizes decentralized wastewater management, sustainable agricultural practices, and community participation.
• Initial phases successfully addressed pollution from municipal sewage and industrial effluents along the main river.
• The long-term vision is to ensure the ecological flow, biodiversity, and cultural heritage of the entire Ganga river basin.
• This comprehensive approach aims for a 'Nirmal' (clean) and 'Aviral' (uninterrupted flow) Ganga and its tributaries.

articles.headline: India's First Private Sector Missile Sub-system Manufacturing Facility Inaugurated
articles.explainer:
• India's first private sector facility for manufacturing critical missile sub-systems has been inaugurated, marking a milestone in defense production.
• The facility will produce advanced components for various missile programs, reducing import dependency and boosting indigenous capabilities.
• This aligns with the government's 'Make in India' initiative and aims to integrate the private sector into defense manufacturing.
• It is expected to create jobs, foster technological innovation, and strengthen India's defense industrial base.
• The move signals growing confidence in the private sector's ability to contribute to strategic defense projects.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA), consider the following statements:
1. IORA was established to promote regional cooperation and sustainable development within the Indian Ocean region.
2. India is a founding member of IORA and has served as its Chair.
3. All member states of IORA are also members of the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC).
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation:
Statement 1 is correct: IORA was established to promote regional cooperation and sustainable development within the Indian Ocean region. Its objectives include promoting sustained growth and balanced development within the IORA region.
Statement 2 is correct: India is a founding member of IORA and has been actively involved in its activities, including chairing the association.
Statement 3 is incorrect: While some countries like India, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, and Thailand are members of both IORA and BIMSTEC, not all IORA members are part of BIMSTEC (e.g., Australia, South Africa, UAE are IORA members but not BIMSTEC members). BIMSTEC focuses specifically on the Bay of Bengal littoral and adjacent regions.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following are the key objectives of the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan?
1. Integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure projects.
2. Reducing logistics costs and improving supply chain efficiency.
3. Promoting multimodal connectivity across various modes of transport.
4. Providing direct financial assistance to individual infrastructure developers.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan's core objectives are indeed integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure projects (1), reducing logistics costs and improving supply chain efficiency (2), and promoting multimodal connectivity (3). It is a digital platform to bring 16 ministries together for integrated planning. Statement 4 is incorrect as Gati Shakti is primarily a planning and coordination tool for infrastructure development, not a scheme for direct financial assistance to individual developers, though it facilitates investment.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023:
1. It provides for the establishment of a Data Protection Board of India.
2. It grants individuals the 'right to be forgotten', allowing them to request the erasure of their personal data.
3. It applies only to online personal data and not to digitized offline data.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation:
Statement 1 is correct: The Act provides for the establishment of the Data Protection Board of India to enforce its provisions.
Statement 2 is correct: The Act includes the 'right to erasure' or 'right to be forgotten', allowing data principals to request the deletion of their personal data.
Statement 3 is incorrect: The Act applies to the processing of digital personal data within India, whether collected online or digitized offline. It also applies to processing outside India if it is for offering goods or services to data principals in India.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a component or objective of India's National Health Mission (NHM)?
options: (b) Reducing maternal and child mortality rates.
(c) Prevention and control of communicable and non-communicable diseases.
(d) Providing universal free health insurance for all citizens above 60 years of age.
prelimsMocks.answer: (d) Providing universal free health insurance for all citizens above 60 years of age.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements (a), (b), and (c) are all core components and objectives of the National Health Mission (NHM), which includes both the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and National Urban Health Mission (NUHM). NHM focuses on strengthening healthcare systems, improving RMNCH+A (Reproductive, Maternal, Neonatal, Child Health + Adolescent Health) outcomes, and controlling various diseases. Statement (d) describes a specific health insurance policy (e.g., under Ayushman Bharat for certain categories, or other senior citizen schemes), but not a universal, free health insurance for all citizens above 60 as a direct, overarching objective or component of NHM itself, though NHM contributes to the broader health ecosystem that may include such schemes.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's renewable energy targets, consider the following statements:
1. India's current commitment includes achieving 50% cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuel-based energy resources by 2030.
2. The International Solar Alliance (ISA) was launched jointly by India and France.
3. The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. India's updated Nationally Determined Contribution (NDC) submitted to the UNFCCC commits to achieving 50% cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuel-based energy resources by 2030 (Statement 1). The International Solar Alliance (ISA) was indeed launched jointly by India and France at the COP21 in Paris (Statement 2). The National Green Hydrogen Mission's primary objective is to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production, utilization, and export, aligning with energy transition and net-zero goals (Statement 3).

mainsMocks.question: India's ambitious renewable energy targets present both significant opportunities and formidable challenges for its energy security and climate diplomacy. Discuss.

mainsMocks.question: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, aims to establish a comprehensive legal framework for data governance in India. Critically examine its provisions in light of the right to privacy and the challenges of its implementation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies; Right to Privacy; GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the context and key features of the DPDP Act. Analyze how it attempts to balance individual privacy with legitimate state and commercial interests. Critically examine specific provisions (e.g., data fiduciary obligations, data principal rights, government exemptions, Data Protection Board) in relation to fundamental rights, particularly the right to privacy (Puttaswamy judgment). Discuss implementation challenges such as compliance burden for businesses, technological infrastructure, awareness among citizens, and effective grievance redressal. Conclude with suggestions for robust implementation and potential areas of refinement.

mainsMocks.question: India's 'Make in India' initiative in the defense sector is crucial for achieving strategic autonomy and boosting economic growth. In this context, discuss the significance of private sector participation in manufacturing advanced defense systems and the challenges it faces.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Science and Technology- indigenization of technology and developing new technology; Security challenges and their management in border areas; Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce 'Make in India' in defense and its objectives. Explain the significance of private sector participation for strategic autonomy (reducing import dependency, self-reliance), economic growth (job creation, technology transfer, R&D), and enhancing defense capabilities. Discuss challenges such as complex procurement procedures, limited R&D funding, technology absorption, intellectual property rights issues, market access for private players, and competition from public sector undertakings. Conclude with policy recommendations to further incentivize and streamline private sector involvement.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Petitions Challenging Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court is set to hear a batch of petitions challenging various provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023.
• Petitioners argue that certain clauses infringe upon fundamental rights, particularly the right to privacy and freedom of speech.
• Key concerns include the broad powers granted to the government for exemptions and the grievance redressal mechanism.
• The government maintains that the Act balances individual rights with legitimate state interests and promotes a safe digital ecosystem.
• This hearing is crucial for clarifying the legal framework for data governance in India.

Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
Right to Privacy

Fundamental Rights involved: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty), Article 19 (Freedom of Speech and Expression).
Puttaswamy Judgement (2017) on Right to Privacy.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the balance struck by the DPDP Act between individual privacy rights and state/commercial interests.
Discuss the potential implications of the Supreme Court's decision on India's digital economy and governance.
Examine the challenges in implementing a comprehensive data protection framework in a diverse digital landscape like India's.
Compare India's DPDP Act with global data protection regulations like GDPR.

GS2.trivia: The concept of 'Right to be forgotten' is a key provision in data protection laws globally, allowing individuals to request deletion of their personal data under certain conditions.

GS2.headline: India Hosts 5th IORA Summit, Emphasizes Maritime Security and Blue Economy
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted the 5th Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA) Summit, bringing together member states for high-level discussions.
• A key focus was enhancing maritime security, combating piracy, and ensuring freedom of navigation in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• Discussions also centered on promoting the 'Blue Economy' through sustainable exploitation of marine resources and ocean governance.
• India reiterated its commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, aligning with its SAGAR (Security And Growth for All in the Region) vision.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration outlining collaborative efforts on disaster management, renewable ocean energy, and cultural exchanges.

• The government argues that the amendments are necessary to balance conservation with the demands of infrastructure development and economic growth.
• Public consultations are underway, inviting feedback from stakeholders before finalization.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns, Focus on Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged in its latest review.
• This decision comes as retail inflation shows signs of moderation, reducing immediate pressure for rate hikes.
• The MPC's stance reflects a continued focus on supporting economic growth while ensuring inflation remains within the target band.
• Analysts suggest that global economic uncertainties and domestic demand conditions influenced the cautious approach.
• Future policy actions will depend on evolving inflation trajectories, global commodity prices, and domestic growth impulses.

GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in India in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in fixing the monetary policy.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Landing System for Future Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a critical test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) landing system.
• The test involved a precise autonomous landing demonstration, a crucial step towards developing a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) RLV.
• This technology aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches by enabling repeated use of rocket components.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) program is a key part of India's vision for future space exploration and commercial space services.
• Successful development will enhance India's competitiveness in the global space market and support human spaceflight missions like Gaganyaan.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology

GS3.trivia: The concept of reusable rockets was pioneered by SpaceX with its Falcon 9, significantly reducing launch costs and increasing launch frequency.

GS3.headline: Project Tiger Celebrates 52 Years, New Census Shows Steady Growth in Tiger Population
GS3.explainer:
• Project Tiger, India's flagship wildlife conservation program, marked its 52nd anniversary, demonstrating remarkable success.
• The latest tiger census data, released by the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA), indicates a steady increase in the wild tiger population.
• This growth is attributed to stringent anti-poaching measures, habitat restoration, and community involvement in conservation efforts.
• The success of Project Tiger is vital for maintaining ecological balance and preserving biodiversity in India's forests.
• Challenges remain, including habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, and the need for continuous monitoring and protection.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment

GS3.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger walking through dense green forest undergrowth, representing successful conservation efforts.
GS3.trivia: India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, making its conservation efforts globally significant.

GS3.headline: Gati Shakti Master Plan Accelerates Infrastructure Development, Boosts Logistics Efficiency
GS3.explainer:
• The PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan has significantly accelerated the pace of multi-modal infrastructure development across India.
• The integrated platform facilitates coordinated planning and execution of projects, reducing delays and cost overruns.
• This initiative aims to improve logistics efficiency, enhance connectivity, and reduce transportation costs for businesses.
• Key achievements include faster approvals, optimal resource utilization, and identification of critical infrastructure gaps.
• The plan is crucial for boosting India's economic competitiveness and achieving its vision of becoming a $5 trillion economy.

GS3.trivia: The Gati Shakti platform integrates 16 ministries, including Railways, Roads, Petroleum & Gas, Power, Telecom, Shipping, and Aviation, for synchronized project planning and execution.

GS3.headline: Indian Army Inducts Advanced Indigenous Artillery Systems, Boosts Border Preparedness
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Army has formally inducted several advanced indigenous artillery gun systems, bolstering its operational capabilities.
• These systems, developed under the 'Make in India' initiative, enhance firepower and precision strike capabilities, particularly in mountainous terrain.
• The induction is part of a broader push towards defense indigenization and reducing reliance on foreign arms imports.
• It significantly strengthens India's preparedness along its borders, especially the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• This move underscores India's commitment to self-reliance in defense manufacturing and modernizing its armed forces.

GS3.trivia: The Advanced Towed Artillery Gun System (ATAGS) is an indigenous 155mm, 52 calibre artillery gun developed by DRDO, which holds the world record for the longest range in its class.

GS3.headline: Fintech Sector Sees Robust Growth, Regulatory Sandbox Expands Scope
GS3.explainer:
• India's Fintech sector continues its robust growth trajectory, driven by digital adoption and innovative solutions.
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has expanded the scope of its regulatory sandbox to foster further innovation in emerging areas.
• This expansion aims to test new products and services in a controlled environment, ensuring consumer protection and financial stability.
• Key growth drivers include digital payments, lending, wealth management, and insurance tech (insurtech).
• India's digital public infrastructure (UPI, Aadhaar) provides a strong foundation for Fintech expansion and financial inclusion.

GS3.headline: Coastal Erosion Mitigation Efforts Intensify Along Indian Coastline
GS3.explainer:
• Government agencies have intensified efforts to combat coastal erosion, which poses a significant threat to India's coastline and coastal communities.
• New projects involving natural solutions like mangrove plantations and artificial reefs are being implemented alongside engineered structures.
• Coastal erosion is exacerbated by rising sea levels, extreme weather events, and unsustainable human activities.
• The initiatives aim to protect vital infrastructure, preserve biodiversity, and safeguard the livelihoods of coastal populations.
• Integrated Coastal Zone Management (ICZM) plans are being updated to address climate change impacts more effectively.

GS3.headline: India's AI Mission Focuses on Ethical AI Development and Skilling Initiatives
GS3.explainer:
• The India AI Mission is increasingly emphasizing ethical considerations in the development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence.
• Policy discussions are centered on creating a framework for responsible AI, addressing concerns like bias, transparency, and accountability.
• Simultaneously, the mission is boosting skilling initiatives to equip the workforce with AI-related competencies.
• The goal is to leverage AI for economic growth and social good while mitigating potential risks and ensuring inclusivity.
• India aims to become a global hub for ethical and responsible AI innovation, aligning with its digital leadership ambitions.

GS3.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing Attracts Major Investment
GS3.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage has successfully attracted significant domestic and international investments.
• This scheme aims to establish giga-factories for battery manufacturing in India, reducing import dependence for critical components.
• It is crucial for India's electric vehicle (EV) transition, renewable energy storage, and achieving energy security goals.
• The Ministry of Heavy Industries reported substantial progress in project implementation and capacity building.
• The PLI scheme is a cornerstone of India's 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives in strategic sectors.

GS3.headline: Ganga Rejuvenation Efforts Enter Phase III, Focus on Tributaries and Sustainable Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Namami Gange Programme has entered its third phase, expanding its focus beyond the main stem of the Ganga to its tributaries.
• The new phase emphasizes decentralized wastewater management, sustainable agricultural practices, and community participation.
• Initial phases successfully addressed pollution from municipal sewage and industrial effluents along the main river.
• The long-term vision is to ensure the ecological flow, biodiversity, and cultural heritage of the entire Ganga river basin.
• This comprehensive approach aims for a 'Nirmal' (clean) and 'Aviral' (uninterrupted flow) Ganga and its tributaries.

GS3.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populated river basin in the world, supporting over 500 million people and covering 11 states in India.

GS3.headline: India's First Private Sector Missile Sub-system Manufacturing Facility Inaugurated
GS3.explainer:
• India's first private sector facility for manufacturing critical missile sub-systems has been inaugurated, marking a milestone in defense production.
• The facility will produce advanced components for various missile programs, reducing import dependency and boosting indigenous capabilities.
• This aligns with the government's 'Make in India' initiative and aims to integrate the private sector into defense manufacturing.
• It is expected to create jobs, foster technological innovation, and strengthen India's defense industrial base.
• The move signals growing confidence in the private sector's ability to contribute to strategic defense projects.
